title: quay.io does not have tagged v1.8.0 skopeo image
body:
skopeo v1.8.0 seems to be missing container images on quay.io:

```
➜  ~ skopeo list-tags docker://quay.io/containers/skopeo
{
    "Repository": "quay.io/containers/skopeo",
    "Tags": [
        "v0.1.42-dev",
        "v1.0.0",
        "v1.1.0",
        "v1.1.1",
        "v1.2.3",
        "v1.2.0-s390x",
        "v1.2.0-amd64",
        "v1.2.0-ppc64le",
        "v1.2.0",
        "v1.3.0",
        "master",
        "v1.3.1",
        "v1.4.0",
        "v1.4.1",
        "v1.5.0",
        "v1.5.1",
        "v1.5.2",
        "v1.6.0",
        "v1.7.0",
        "latest"
    ]
}
➜  ~ skopeo list-tags docker://quay.io/skopeo/stable
{
    "Repository": "quay.io/skopeo/stable",
    "Tags": [
        "v0.1.42-dev",
        "v1.0.0",
        "v1.1.0",
        "v1.1.1",
        "v1.2.3",
        "v1.2.0-s390x",
        "v1.2.0-amd64",
        "v1.2.0-ppc64le",
        "v1.2.0",
        "v1.3.0",
        "master",
        "v1.3.1",
        "v1.4.0",
        "v1.4.1",
        "v1.5.0",
        "v1.5.1",
        "v1.5.2",
        "v1.6.0",
        "v1.7.0",
        "latest"
    ]
}
```

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

The stable image is based on Fedora stable packaging, which is at 1.7.0 per https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/skopeo . @lsm5 is that expected? I see 1.8 commits on https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/skopeo/commits/f36 , but no erratum on https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/?search=skopeo .
that's my bad, looks like the autobuilder job failed to actually do the builds. I'm triggering them now.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This should be fixed now. v1.8.0-8 is in stable on f36. Please reopen if anything.
